title: Exquisite Creamy Chaozhou Spaghetti Wonton Soup
description: A fatty and creamy Chaozhou dish featuring spaghetti and wontons, perfect for a sick day, and is halal-friendly.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti according to the package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and saute the sliced ginger and minced garlic until fragrant.
Add the ground pork to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook until pink and fully cooked.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked ground pork and shrimp mixture, and mix well with soy sauce.
Lay a wonton wrapper on a clean surface and place a small spoonful of the pork and shrimp mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the wonton wrapper, then fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point.
Repeat the process with the remaining wonton wrappers and filling.
In a large pot, combine the chicken broth and coconut milk, and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Add the cooked spaghetti and wontons to the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the wontons are cooked through and float to the surface.
